narrative.headline: What 2014 Jeep Cherokee owners report most
narrative.summary: The dominant pattern in owner complaints centers on the power train, where transmission-related noises, fluid leaks, and shifting problems are frequently described. Engine and electrical system faults appear alongside warning lights and sudden loss of drive, sometimes with transmission warnings surfacing immediately after routine service. A smaller share of owners report fuel and propulsion irregularities, notably violent shaking under hard acceleration.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report fluid spots appearing beneath the parked vehicle, followed by a pronounced whining noise and progressively worsening transmission behavior. · 7 fire · 19 crash · 9 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report a SERVICE TRANSMISSION warning appearing on the dashboard shortly after routine oil and filter service, while driving on a highway. · 7 fire · 7 crash · 7 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report multiple warning lights illuminating simultaneously during low-speed acceleration, accompanied by hard shifting, a locked steering wheel, battery warnings, and the vehicle stalling with the inability to shift gears. · 9 fire · 6 crash · 6 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report severe side-to-side shaking of the vehicle when accelerating hard from a complete stop, described as similar to a wheel being out of balance. · 1 fire · 1 injured
